diagrams.title: 8-Hour Course Timeline and Topic Flow
diagrams.description: A horizontal timeline showing the progression of topics across 8 hours with connections between concepts
elements: Arrow to Hour 2-3: Orthogonality → Orthogonal Sets
Arrow to Hour 7: Least Squares Problems
Arrow to Hour 8: Review and Applications
Side annotations showing key connections between non-consecutive topics
diagrams.purpose: Shows students the logical progression and interconnections, reducing perceived fragmentation of topics

diagrams.title: Three-Section Board Layout
diagrams.description: Visual representation of how to organize the blackboard during chalk and talk teaching
elements: Top section (20%): Key definitions in boxes - Inner Product, Norm, Orthogonal
Middle section (60%): Current derivation - Gram-Schmidt formulas with annotations
Bottom section (20%): Previous results for reference - theorems, earlier examples
Color coding suggested: blue for definitions, green for theorems, red for current work
diagrams.purpose: Provides teachers with a template for organized board work that aids student comprehension and reference

diagrams.title: Concept Dependency Map for Inner Product Spaces
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing prerequisites and dependencies between concepts in the course
elements: Third level: Cauchy-Schwarz, Triangle Inequality, Orthogonal Sets
Fifth level: QR Factorization, Least Squares
diagrams.purpose: Helps instructors identify which concepts must be taught first and where students might face difficulties due to missing prerequisites
